title: Stachys integrifolia
taxonomy: Stachys integrifolia is classified under the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flowering patterns. Stachys integrifolia is further categorized within the genus Stachys, which consists of over 300 species of plants. The genus Stachys is part of the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and rosemary. Stachys integrifolia's classification is a reflection of its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Lamiaceae family.

intro: Stachys integrifolia, also known as woundwort or knotted hedge nettle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Stachys integrifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stachys integrifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used topically to support wound healing and internally to promote overall health and well-being.
